user_prompt: Write for me `lib\ExtCodec\ExtCodec.cpp` and its associated header file. Please follow the guidelines `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md` as you write the code
There will be only one instance, make it a namespaced module, and it will work tightly with `AudioManager`
The codec is the `SGTL5000`, and I have added the files `lib\ExtCodec\control_sgtl5000.cpp` and `lib\ExtCodec\control_sgtl5000.h` to help this effort get started.

There will be a initialization function. This will first check the I2C address to see if it responds, and the rest of `AudioManager` and `RecordingView`behavior will depend on if it is available. This is to be done while the splash screen is still happening. Later we might even update the splash screen to indicate if an external CODEC is connected.
there should be a state enum like
GPIO35 is an ADC capable pin and is dedicated to sensing if a earbud is connected, a high-ish voltage means connected
GPIO36 is an ADC capable pin and is dedicated to sensing if a earbud has an inline mic, a low-ish voltage would indicate there’s no mic (as it will be shorted to HP-GND physically)

If the external codec is connected, then we will start up a dedicated ADC thread. It doesn’t need much stack. All it does is do ADC reads once every 100ms, blocking yet yielding. It sets the state of the module accordingly and maybe set an event flag. Other threads will use the state change to reconfigure electrically and reconfigure the GUI accordingly
Another mic is connected to line-in-right of the `SGTL5000` and it will be used if the earbud-inline-mic is not available
Remember that the `SGTL5000` will use stereo and a different BCLK pin for the I2S. The entire I2S does need to be reconfigured when we are switching between the SGTL5000 and the NS4168.
So in our current implementation, we are already operating under `EXTCODEC_UNAVAIL`. No change in behavior needed

Under state `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD` we still have push-to-talk behavior, but instead of switching to the `SPM1423` when we push-to-talk, we switch to the `SGTL5000` using line-in-right as the sound source.

Under `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD` we have bidirectional audio, the mic is using line-in-right, do not use push-to-talk behavior, we can mute the mic, we can mute the sound output, but now changing mic state doesn’t change sound output state, and changing sound output state doesn’t change mic state

Under `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC` we have bidirectional audio, the mic is using the `SGTL5000`'s dedicated mic input, do not use push-to-talk behavior, we can mute the mic, we can mute the sound output, but now changing mic state doesn’t change sound output state, and changing sound output state doesn’t change mic state

Add to `inc\pins.h`, the I2S_BCLK pin for the SGTL5000 will be GPIO13, the I2S_MCLK pin will be GPIO14 (and only used if the external codec is detected). GPIO34 is for the I2S data for the mic. GPIO2 is for the I2S data for the earbud. GPIO0 is for the I2S_LRCLK.
`AudioFifo` already has special enqueue and dequeue functions for handling stereo data, if these are missing then implement them
agent_reply: Created [ExtCodec.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.h:1) and [ExtCodec.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:1) as a namespaced singleton-style module for SGTL5000 detection/state. It checks I2C address `0x0A`, initializes SGTL5000 control if present, starts a small ADC monitor task, tracks the four requested states, exposes state/event helpers, and has analog-path helpers for line-in-right vs dedicated mic.

Also added the SGTL5000/I2S/sense pins in [pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16), added tiny compatibility shims [AudioControl.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/AudioControl.h:1) and [AudioStream.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/AudioStream.h:1) so the starter SGTL5000 control files build, and call `ExtCodec::init()` during splash boot in [main.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:126).

I intentionally did not switch `AudioManager` into external-I2S routing yet, since its external I2S path is still stubbed and you asked current `EXTCODEC_UNAVAIL` behavior to remain unchanged. `AudioFifo` already had stereo enqueue/dequeue functions, so I left that alone.

#10 0x400e4d19 in m5::I2C_Device::readRegister(unsigned char, unsigned char*, unsigned int) const at .pio/libdeps/thefly_sec0/M5Unified/src/utility/imu/../I2C_Class.hpp:187
that's new, probably the codec checking and the battery tracker conflicting?
agent_reply: Yep, I think that was it. Not really codec ADC vs battery tracker, but the new codec code was using raw Arduino `Wire` on the same internal I2C bus that `M5.Power.getBatteryVoltage()` uses through M5Unified. The bare `Wire.begin()` in the SGTL5000 enable path was especially suspicious.
I patched it so the codec now shares M5Unified’s I2C path:
- [ExtCodec.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:258) probes with `M5.In_I2C.scanID()` and no longer calls `Wire.begin()`.
- Removed raw `Wire` use from the ExtCodec path.
One note: the builds regenerated `src/version.c`; `tools/test_builds/` is still untracked from before.

user_prompt: have a if statement that, if MCLK is not configured, then make the SGTL5000 use the mode where it derives a MCLK from BCLK, I think that is possible
agent_reply: I added the `if` guard, but I did not fake a BCLK-derived MCLK mode because the SGTL5000 does not appear to have one.
In [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:315), if `kSGTL5000I2sMclk < 0`, bring-up now fails with:
SGTL5000 SYS_MCLK is not configured; this codec cannot derive SYS_MCLK from I2S BCLK

Reason: the SGTL5000 datasheet describes PLL input as an 8-27 MHz system clock on `SYS_MCLK`, and says that in I2S slave mode LRCLK/SCLK must be synchronous to `SYS_MCLK`. That is not the same as an ES8311-style “MCLK=BCLK” register mode. Source: NXP SGTL5000 datasheet, especially the PLL/system-clock feature and I2S slave clocking notes: https://www.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/SGTL5000.pdf?pspll=1
Validated with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`. `git diff --check` is clean except the repo’s usual CRLF warnings, and I restored `src/version.c` after the build.

commit_message: NS4168 works on stereo, added SGTL5000 config for our I2S format

user_prompt: is `analogRead` yielding internally when it waits for the reading?
agent_reply: No, not in the usual useful sense.

In the Arduino ESP32 core this project is building against, `analogRead()` goes through `__analogReadRaw()` and then calls either `adc1_get_raw()` or `adc2_get_raw()`. There is no `yield()`, `vTaskDelay()`, or `taskYIELD()` in the Arduino `analogRead()` path itself.
For your current `ExtCodec.cpp` case, the sense pins are GPIO35 and GPIO36, which are ADC1 pins. So the path is basically:

A small nuance: ADC1 may block if another ADC1 user holds the ADC lock, and that lock wait is FreeRTOS-backed, so it can yield while waiting for the lock. But the actual conversion read is synchronous; it is not politely yielding between samples.
ADC2 is different: if Wi-Fi owns ADC2, `adc2_get_raw()` tends to fail/timeout instead of patiently waiting. That does not apply to GPIO35/GPIO36.

So for your polling code: the explicit `vTaskDelay()` between polls is the real cooperative yield. The 4-sample `analogRead()` loop itself is effectively a short blocking burst. Given it is only a handful of ADC1 reads every 100 ms, that should be fine unless you see measurable scheduler jitter.

ADC2 is different: if Wi-Fi owns ADC2, `adc2_get_raw()` tends to fail/timeout instead of patiently waiting. That does not apply to GPIO35/GPIO36.

So for your polling code: the explicit `vTaskDelay()` between polls is the real cooperative yield. The 4-sample `analogRead()` loop itself is effectively a short blocking burst. Given it is only a handful of ADC1 reads every 100 ms, that should be fine unless you see measurable scheduler jitter.

user_prompt: I modified `lib\ExtCodec\ExtCodec.cpp` to express what I really want, instead of an averaging filter, I want just agreement across samples, review my change
agent_reply: High: [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:382) reads `kExtCodecEarbudSenseAdc` in the inline-mic loop. That means once an earbud is detected, the second loop is still reading the earbud sense pin, and because connected earbud values are above `1800`, they will also be above the inline mic threshold `800`. Result: real hardware will likely classify every connected earbud as `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC`.

High: [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:353) through [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:360) has no `break` or `return` in the mock `switch`. If `TEST_MOCK_EXT_CODEC` is enabled, every case falls through to `default`, so the final state is always `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD`.

Medium: [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:373) and [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:384) do not really implement “agreement across samples.” The earbud path treats one low sample as no earbud, and the inline-mic path treats one high sample as mic present. That is closer to “any disagreeing earbud sample disconnects, any agreeing mic sample wins” than sample agreement. For true agreement, I’d expect all samples to vote the same before changing state, with mixed samples probably leaving the previous state alone.

I didn’t edit anything. The shape I’d patch toward is: sample each ADC pin into boolean votes, require unanimous earbud-present before considering mic state, require unanimous mic-present or mic-absent before choosing the two earbud states, and keep the previous state when samples disagree.

agent_reply: Medium: [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:388) still does not require agreement for inline mic present. One high sample immediately sets `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC`; the other three samples do not have to agree. A single ADC spike above `800` can flip the state to “with mic.”

Medium: [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:377) is also asymmetric for earbud detection. One low sample immediately sets `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD`; the other samples do not get a chance to agree. If “agreement across samples” means unanimous samples before changing state, mixed earbud samples should probably leave the previous state unchanged rather than forcing no-earbud.
The two previous concrete bugs are fixed: the inline-mic loop now reads `kExtCodecInlineMicSenseAdc`, and the mock `switch` now has `break`s.

I’d shape this as “read N threshold votes, then only change state if the votes are unanimous.” Mixed votes become “no confident new state,” so `poll_adc_state()` just preserves the current state for that poll.

user_prompt: I hear your concerns but those are exactly what I intend, but you said the word asymmetric so it got me thinking...
can you change the code so that 4 consecutive readings that are "the same bracket" is needed to change out of whatever state it is currently in?
agent_reply: The ADC polling now treats each paired earbud/inline-mic ADC read as one “bracket” sample, then only changes state after 4 consecutive samples in the same bracket that differs from the current state. If samples bounce between brackets, the pending change resets. If samples match the current state, the pending change is cleared.

I also kept your mock-state switch fix intact and removed the averaging helper. Validation: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed, and `git diff --check` is clean except for the usual CRLF warning on `ExtCodec.cpp`.

commit_message: testing to see if SGTL5000 shows up on I2C

user_prompt: add a function to ExtCodec that checks if the SGTL5000's PLL is locked
agent_reply: Added `ExtCodec::pllLocked()` in [ExtCodec.h](<d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.h:42>) and implemented it in [ExtCodec.cpp](<d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:206>).

It reads SGTL5000 `CHIP_ANA_STATUS` register `0x0036` and checks bit 4, `PLL_IS_LOCKED`. It uses the same `M5.In_I2C` transaction style as the SGTL5000 control code and returns `false` if the register read fails. For `TEST_MOCK_EXT_CODEC`, it returns `true`.
Build passes, and `src/version.c` was restored after PlatformIO regenerated it.
Done. I updated [test_sgtl5000_pll_lock.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_sgtl5000_pll_lock.cpp:148) so it now prints useful live telemetry instead of only printing on a PLL-loss event.
I also added `ExtCodec::readChipAnaStatus()` in [ExtCodec.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.h:43), using the same internal I2C register-read path as `pllLocked()`.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: build passed. `src/version.c` is clean again.

commit_message: volume ceiling, and went back to using software vol control
